title: Route-aware diesel stop planning (bounded composite, multi-tool)
source: Tango jobs manual test checklist: 'Diesel station search' (travel.diesel_lookup, untested end-to-end); the user 2026-06-09: diesel is tricky because it composes current location, route reading, and price lookup
system: You are Sierra, a careful travel-planning assistant. Use tools when a factual route, drive-time, fuel-price, or detour claim depends on real data. Never invent station names or prices. Label tool-backed facts separately from synthesis.
prompt: I'm driving my diesel truck from Florence, Oregon to Boise, Idaho tomorrow. Find me good diesel options near Boise for when I arrive — best price with minimal detour — and tell me whether it's worth fueling somewhere along the way instead. Use real current prices, not guesses, and give me the top picks with addresses.
successCriteria: Calls find_diesel with the trip destination and grounds every station/price claim in its output.
Reports real station names, addresses, and current prices from the tool — nothing invented.
Considers detour distance, not just price, when ranking picks.
Answers the along-the-route question honestly — either with a tool-backed comparison or by saying it wasn't checked.
Gives an actionable recommendation (top picks with addresses).
knownFailureModes: Quotes plausible-sounding diesel prices or station names without calling find_diesel.
Ignores detour mileage and recommends purely on price.
Claims to have compared en-route options without any supporting tool call.
Dumps raw tool output without a recommendation.

rubric.name: price_grounding
rubric.description: All station names and prices come from find_diesel output; no fabricated fuel data.

rubric.name: route_awareness
rubric.description: Detour miles and arrival-area framing factor into the recommendation; the en-route question is handled with evidence or honest uncertainty.

rubric.name: user_utility
rubric.description: Clear top picks with addresses and a price/detour tradeoff the user can act on.

rubric.name: honesty
rubric.description: Tool-verified facts clearly separated from synthesis; uncertainty acknowledged where not checked.
